# sourcevision

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 141
- **Lines of code**: 45,234
- **Languages**: TypeScript (128), JSON (7), Markdown (3), Other (3)
- **Zones**: 9 (9 with descriptions)
- **Git**: main @ 63d150a
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-05T14:57:14.728Z

## Architecture Zones

### Analysis Engine (`analysis-engine`)

Core multi-phase analysis pipeline responsible for enriching inventory, resolving imports, detecting zones, and producing AI-consumable output artifacts.

- Files: 39
- Cohesion: 0.67, Coupling: 0.33
- Entry points: src/analyzers/callgraph-findings.ts, src/analyzers/claude-client.ts, src/analyzers/context.ts, src/analyzers/enrich-parsing.ts, src/analyzers/enrich.ts (+11 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/analysis-engine/context.md`

### Branch Work Analytics (`branch-work-analytics`)

Branch-scoped work tracking, risk scoring, workspace aggregation, schema definitions, MCP server, and PR markdown generation — the data layer and workspace-level analytics surface.

- Files: 28
- Cohesion: 0.57, Coupling: 0.43
- Entry points: src/analyzers/branch-work-classifier.ts, src/analyzers/risk-scoring.ts, src/cli/commands/workspace.ts, src/cli/mcp.ts, src/generators/pr-markdown-template.ts (+3 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/branch-work-analytics/context.md`

### Claude IDE Config (`claude-ide-config`)

Local Claude Code IDE settings and permission overrides for the project.

- Files: 2
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/claude-ide-config/context.md`

### CLI Infrastructure (`cli-infrastructure`)

CLI command implementations, manifest management, output formatting, error handling, and project constants that form the user-facing interface layer of sourcevision.

- Files: 26
- Cohesion: 0.71, Coupling: 0.29
- Entry points: src/analyzers/manifest.ts, src/cli/commands/init.ts, src/cli/commands/pr-markdown.ts, src/cli/errors.ts, src/cli/output.ts (+1 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/cli-infrastructure/context.md`

### File Analysis Pipeline (`file-analysis-pipeline`)

Houses the individual analyzer modules (callgraph, components, imports, inventory, route detection) that each handle one phase of codebase introspection.

- Files: 11
- Cohesion: 0.54, Coupling: 0.46
- Entry points: src/analyzers/callgraph.ts, src/analyzers/components.ts, src/analyzers/imports.ts, src/analyzers/inventory.ts, src/util/paths.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/file-analysis-pipeline/context.md`

### Project Root (`project-root`)

Root-level documentation, workspace configuration, and build tooling that anchor the monorepo.

- Files: 10
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/project-root/context.md`

### Schema Validation (`schema-validation`)

Groups the schema validation module with the end-to-end and integration test suite, reflecting that validation logic is the primary invariant exercised across the full analysis pipeline.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 0.43, Coupling: 0.57
- Entry points: src/schema/validate.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/schema-validation/context.md`

### Test Fixture Projects (`test-fixture-projects`)

Self-contained sample projects (a Remix app and a small TypeScript project) used as realistic inputs for end-to-end and integration tests of the analysis pipeline.

- Files: 11
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/test-fixture-projects/context.md`

### Zone Detection Engine (`zone-detection-engine`)

Encapsulates the Louvain community-detection algorithm, zone hashing for stable IDs, and the zones orchestrator that turns import graphs into labelled architectural zones.

- Files: 6
- Cohesion: 0.43, Coupling: 0.57
- Entry points: src/analyzers/zone-hash.ts, src/analyzers/zones.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/zone-detection-engine/context.md`

## Key Dependencies

### Most-Imported Files

- `src/cli/output.ts` (14 imports)
- `src/util/sort.ts` (12 imports)
- `src/analyzers/claude-client.ts` (11 imports)
- `src/cli/errors.ts` (11 imports)
- `src/schema/data-files.ts` (10 imports)
- `src/analyzers/enrich-parsing.ts` (9 imports)
- `src/util/paths.ts` (9 imports)
- `src/analyzers/zones.ts` (8 imports)
- `src/analyzers/inventory.ts` (7 imports)
- `src/analyzers/token-usage.ts` (7 imports)

### External Packages

- `vitest` (used by 53 files)
- `@n-dx/llm-client` (used by 23 files)
- `typescript` (used by 5 files)
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (used by 2 files)
- `zod` (used by 2 files)
- `ora` (used by 1 files)
- `pdfkit` (used by 1 files)

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- analysis-engine → branch-work-analytics has 6 outbound imports in addition to the 27 inbound, creating a 33-crossing bidirectional pair — the highest cross-zone traffic volume in the package; this volume indicates the two zones share mutable state or type definitions that belong in a shared foundation, not in either consumer zone [critical] (analysis-engine)
- claude-client.ts (vendor HTTP adapter) and completion-reader.ts (streaming I/O parser) are infrastructure files embedded in the domain zone; they should be extracted to an infra or adapters layer so the domain zone contains no vendor or transport concerns [warning] (analysis-engine)
- branch-work-collector.ts is homed in cli-infrastructure but its semantics are domain-level (collecting branch work data for analytics); the file is likely misplaced and belongs in branch-work-analytics, causing CLI infrastructure to own domain logic [warning] (branch-work-analytics)
- analysis-engine imports 12 symbols from cli-infrastructure (analyzers → cli: 12), making the domain zone depend on the presentation/CLI layer; this inverts the stability gradient and means CLI refactors can break domain logic — the 12 import sites must be audited and the direction reversed [critical] (cli-infrastructure)
- prd-epic-resolver.ts encodes rex PRD/epic domain concepts inside sourcevision's CLI infrastructure zone; sourcevision must not embed another package's domain vocabulary in its own layers — this file should either live in rex or behind a gateway abstraction [warning] (cli-infrastructure)
- route-detection.ts carries the 'route-handler' archetype but performs passive static analysis of file paths with no HTTP request handling. The incorrect archetype will cause architectural tooling and AI consumers to treat it as request-handling infrastructure rather than a pipeline analyzer. Correct to 'service' or 'utility'. [warning] (file-analysis-pipeline)
- schema-validation aggregates validate.ts with 4 test files at three different abstraction levels in a single community. Zone health metrics are dominated by the test file population, making validate.ts's actual production coupling invisible in aggregate reports. The production file should reside in a dedicated schema zone; the test files should be distributed to their respective test-level zones. [warning] (schema-validation)
- zone-hash.ts is classified 'utility' but accessed directly as an entrypoint by external zones, creating a contradiction between its archetype and its observed import profile. Either correct the archetype to reflect its public role, or collapse it into zones.ts and remove the external import surface to restore its private status. [warning] (zone-detection-engine)
- God function: registerMcpTools in src/cli/mcp.ts calls 36 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]

### Patterns

- analysis-engine has no outbound imports to cli-infrastructure or branch-work-analytics yet both import heavily from it (12 and 27 crossings respectively) — this unidirectional fan-out is healthy and should be preserved as the package grows [info] (analysis-engine)
- cli-infrastructure acts as a bidirectional mediator (consuming and re-providing across both analysis-engine and branch-work-analytics) rather than a clean terminal layer; extracting a shared-types module that both zones import would convert this mediator into a proper sink [warning] (cli-infrastructure)
- validate.ts cohesion is obscured by co-membership with multi-level test files; isolating it in a dedicated schema zone would surface its true coupling profile and make the production schema boundary auditable. [warning] (schema-validation)
- This zone is the only zone in the package with both perfect cohesion and zero coupling — it serves as the cleanliness baseline against which other zones' test discipline can be measured. [info] (test-fixture-projects)
- Zero imports from this zone reach the CLI or schema layers — it is a pure algorithmic leaf with a single consumer (analysis-engine). This clean single-direction dependency should be preserved and documented as a constraint. [info] (zone-detection-engine)

### Relationships

- branch-work-analytics is imported by analyzers-3 (zone-detection-engine) via 3 crossings — a lower-level analyzer zone depending upward into a higher-level consumer zone is a dependency inversion that breaks the expected layering [warning] (branch-work-analytics)
- branch-work-analytics → e2e: 2 imports cross the production/test boundary in the wrong direction; production zone must not import from e2e test zone [critical] (branch-work-analytics)
- The 15-in / 7-out import ratio with the analysis-engine zone is nearly symmetric, indicating no clear dependency direction; establishing a formal contract (one zone depends on the other, not both) would resolve this. [warning] (file-analysis-pipeline)
- e2e tests import 3 symbols directly from file-analysis-pipeline internals, bypassing src/public.ts; this creates a fragile test boundary that will produce silent breakage on internal refactors. [warning] (file-analysis-pipeline)
- Transitive coupling chain exists: branch-work-analytics (unit) → schema-validation (e2e) → file-analysis-pipeline (analyzers-2). Changes in file-analysis-pipeline internals can silently break branch-work-analytics tests via this three-zone indirect path. [warning] (schema-validation)
- Import-graph isolation is confirmed, but runtime isolation (no production code referencing fixture paths via dynamic strings) has not been verified — a lint rule banning fixture path strings in src/ would close this gap. [info] (test-fixture-projects)
- All 10 outbound imports target the analysis-engine zone with nothing flowing back into the CLI layer, making zone-detection-engine the only non-trivial zone with a strictly unidirectional import profile. [info] (zone-detection-engine)

### Observations

- 16 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports [warning] (analysis-engine)
- Bidirectional imports with analyzers-2 (15 in, 7 out) suggest a forced algorithmic split — these zones likely represent a single logical subsystem and should be reviewed for consolidation. [warning] (analysis-engine)
- Cohesion of 0.67 is healthy but 16 entry points create a wide coupling surface; narrowing exports to a facade module would lower downstream coupling. [info] (analysis-engine)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (claude-ide-config)
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling confirm this zone is correctly isolated as pure configuration with no code dependencies. [info] (claude-ide-config)
- Bidirectional coupling: "analysis-engine" ↔ "branch-work-analytics" (6+27 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Hub: src/analyzers/zones.ts is imported by 4 zones — cross-cutting dependency [info]
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 39 files vs smallest with 2 — uneven decomposition [info]
- Fan-in hotspot: src/cli/output.ts receives calls from 13 files (high fan-in expected for this module type) — high-impact module, changes may have wide ripple effects [info]
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (project-root)
- ... and 21 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "analysis-engine" has files across 9 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (analysis-engine)
- Audit archetypes.ts import count across zones — if it is imported by 2+ zones it is already a de-facto cross-cutting hub and should be extracted to the shared-types foundation alongside v1.ts [warning] (analysis-engine)
- Group enrich-batch.ts and enrich-config.ts into a src/analyzers/enrichment/ subdirectory — the shared prefix is an implicit sub-module boundary that should be made structural [info] (analysis-engine)
- Zone "branch-work-analytics" has files across 11 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (branch-work-analytics)
- Flag this zone as high-fragility: cohesion 0.57 + coupling 0.43 is the worst combined score in the package — any PR touching files in this zone has elevated risk of cascading breakage and should require additional review [warning] (branch-work-analytics)
- Move pr-markdown-template.ts to cli-infrastructure alongside pr-markdown.ts — co-locating template with command removes one coupling edge between analytics and CLI without any behavioral change [info] (branch-work-analytics)
- Move src/cli/commands/workspace.ts to cli-infrastructure alongside all other CLI command files — its presence in analytics is the root cause of back-imports from analytics into CLI and is responsible for the bidirectional coupling pair [warning] (branch-work-analytics)
- Zone "cli-infrastructure" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (cli-infrastructure)
- Rename the zone from 'cli-infrastructure' to 'cli-commands' or 'cli-adapters' — 'infrastructure' implies foundational/low-level, but this zone is a high-level consumer of domain zones; the misleading name makes the dependency inversion (analyzers → cli) harder to detect [info] (cli-infrastructure)
- branch-work-collector.ts uses the 'branch-work-' domain prefix while every other file in this zone uses CLI-scoped names — the naming inconsistency is a reliable indicator the file is misplaced; move it to branch-work-analytics where its prefix convention matches [info] (cli-infrastructure)
- ... and 19 more
